title: Executive Summary
content:
GOOGL closes 2025-12-31 with $403B of revenue (15.1% YoY) and $132B of net income (32.0% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 32.0% and a net margin of 32.8%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue growth outpaced margin retention, so earnings improved without the same degree of operating efficiency.

Operating cash flow reached $165B (31.5% YoY), and after $91.4B of capital spending the business produced $73.3B of free cash flow (0.7% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 22.7% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 2.01x and debt-to-equity of 0.12x.

*Snapshot period: FY2025. Prior period: FY2024.*

The dashboard uses the same row order across reports, and values the filing does not disclose remain explicitly marked rather than being hidden.
*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*
*Earnings Per Share (Diluted): Earnings Per Share (Diluted) uses diluted weighted-average shares from the income statement.*
*Return on Assets: ROA uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*

title: Trend Analysis
content:
Across the annual periods shown here, net income provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $132B and a compound annual growth rate of 21.5%. The multi-year progression is steady enough to suggest an improving operating profile. The main trend relationship is therefore one of scale improving faster than margin retention.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $165B of operating cash flow translating into $73.3B of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.12x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (2.01x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

title: Risk Factors
content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. The main areas to monitor remain profitability at 32.0% and free cash flow at $73.3B, because those lines usually determine whether the balance sheet is strengthening or merely holding steady.

title: Analyst Notes
content:
The most constructive part of GOOGL's profile is straightforward: Gross margin of 59.7% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses. ROE of 35.7% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. That matters because leverage (0.12x debt-to-equity), liquidity (2.01x current ratio), and free cash flow ($73.3B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
